title: Mapped types
instruction:
A mapped type creates a new type by iterating over an existing type's keys with `[K in keyof T]`.

Run this.
successMessage: `Stringify<T>` maps over every key and changes the value type to `string`. This is how built-in utilities like `Partial<T>` and `Readonly<T>` work under the hood.

title: Conditional types
instruction:
Conditional types choose between types based on a condition: `T extends U ? TrueType : FalseType`.

Run this.
successMessage: `infer E` extracts the element type from an array type. This is how `ReturnType<T>` and `Parameters<T>` work internally.

title: Template literal types
instruction:
Template literal types combine string literal types to build new string types programmatically.

Run this.
successMessage: Template literal types generate all combinations of the constituent literals. `setSpacing("margin-diagonal", 8)` would be a type error.
